packages/web/components/modals/agent/types.ts defines 'ConnectionMethodType' with the same 6 values
packages/web/lib/agent-factory.ts defines VALID_CONNECTION_METHODS const array and derives 'ConnectionMethodValue' type — same 6 values

detail.suggestion: Use the canonical ConnectionMethod from packages/shared/types/agent.ts everywhere. In agent-factory.ts, import ConnectionMethod and derive VALID_CONNECTION_METHODS from it. review::.::holistic::logic_clarity::put_.summary: PUT /internal/messages validation condition conflates empty-string and undefined checks

evidence: Line 76: `if (!content && content !== '' && !streamingStatus)` — the double-negative `!content && content !== ''` is checking for `undefined` or `null` but not empty string. The intent is to allow empty-string content, but the expression is harder to parse than necessary. A developer reading this must mentally evaluate `!content && content !== ''` to understand it means `content is null or undefined`.
detail.suggestion: Replace `!content && content !== '' && !streamingStatus` with `content === undefined && content === null && !streamingStatus` or use `(content == null && !streamingStatus)` which is the idiomatic JS way to check null/undefined without catching empty string.

review::.::holistic::logic_clarity::serv.summary: POST /api/servers validates `name.length < 1` after already checking `!name` on a trimmed string

evidence: Line 86: `if (!name || name.length < 1 || name.length > 100)` — since `name` is the result of `body.name?.trim()`, the `!name` check already catches empty strings and the `name.length < 1` check is redundant. If `name` is falsy (empty string after trim, null, or undefined), `!name` is true. If `!name` is false, `name.length < 1` can never be true.
detail.suggestion: Simplify to `if (!name || name.length > 100)` — the `name.length < 1` condition is unreachable when `!name` is false.

review::.::holistic::logic_clarity::chan.summary: PATCH /channels/{channelId} performs two sequential channel updates when agentIds is provided

evidence: Lines 215-238: When `agentIds` is in the request body, the handler first runs a transaction that updates the channel's `defaultAgentId` (line 223-228), and then unconditionally runs a second `prisma.channel.update` at line 232 with `updateData`. If the request body contains both `agentIds` and `defaultAgentId`, the second update overwrites the first's defaultAgentId value. Even when `updateData` is empty (only `agentIds` was sent), the second update still executes with an empty `data` object — a wasted DB call.
detail.suggestion: Skip the final `prisma.channel.update` when `Object.keys(updateData).length === 0` and `agentIds` was already handled by the transaction, or merge both updates into a single transaction.

review::.::holistic::type_safety::unvali.summary: use-channel.ts force-casts all Phoenix channel payloads via `as` without runtime validation

evidence: 15+ occurrences of `raw as SomeType` across channel event handlers: message_new (line 276), sync_response (line 283), history_response (line 292), stream_start (line 350), stream_token (line 389), stream_complete (line 409), stream_error (line 443), stream_thinking (line 514), stream_tool_call (line 542), stream_tool_result (line 574), typed_message (line 608), reaction_update (line 616), charter_status (line 665), message_edited (line 687), message_deleted (line 705)

The `raw: unknown` parameter is immediately cast without any shape validation — if the Gateway sends a payload missing expected fields (e.g., no `messageId` in stream_token), the code will silently produce undefined values that propagate through the message state
detail.suggestion: Create a lightweight runtime validator (or use zod) for each channel event payload shape. At minimum, add a guard function that checks required fields exist before casting, e.g., `function isStreamToken(raw: unknown): raw is StreamTokenPayload`. Apply at each channel.on() handler to fail gracefully on malformed payloads.

If a caller passes an invalid triggerMode like 'INVALID', the cast suppresses the type error and the invalid value reaches the Prisma query

review::.::holistic::cross_module_archit.summary: Agent API key authentication (hash + lookup) is implemented independently in three locations: lib/route-handlers.ts (authenticateAgentKey), lib/agent-auth.ts (authenticateApiKey), and app/api/v1/agents/[id]/route.ts (authenticateAgent). Each re-derives the SHA-256 hash and queries AgentRegistration with slightly different signatures and return types.

evidence: route-handlers.ts exports hashApiKey and authenticateAgentKey (line 647). agent-auth.ts has a private authenticateApiKey and exports authenticateAgentRequest + authenticateAgentKey (line 22-91). v1/agents/[id]/route.ts has its own local authenticateAgent function (line 16-43). All three compute crypto.createHash('sha256').update(apiKey).digest('hex') and query agentRegistration.findFirst.
detail.suggestion: Consolidate to a single authenticateAgentKey implementation in lib/agent-auth.ts and import it everywhere. Remove the duplicate in route-handlers.ts and the local copy in v1/agents/[id]/route.ts.
